fundamentals.name: iShares MSCI South Korea ETF
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: The fund generally will invest at least 80% of its assets in the component securities of its underlying index and in investments that have economic characteristics that are substantially identical to the component securities of its underlying index. The index is a free float-adjusted market capitalization-weighted index that is designed to measure the performance of the large- and mid-capitalization segments of the equity market in Korea. The fund is non-diversified.

judgment.summary: An ETF tracking South Korea, EWY shows a high trailing P/E without available forward multiples or growth metrics, indicating potential overvaluation and lack of clear growth drivers.
positives: Trailing P/E of 27.08 suggests some level of profitability in the underlying holdings.
negatives: Forward P/E, PEG ratio, P/B, P/S, and revenue/earnings growth metrics are unavailable, obscuring future prospects.
Margin and return data are missing, preventing assessment of underlying company profitability and efficiency.
Balance sheet and cash flow data are absent, making it impossible to gauge financial health and liquidity.
Analyst targets and Beta are not provided, limiting insight into market expectations and risk.

news.paragraph: The iShares MSCI South Korea ETF (EWY) has seen significant outflows, with record redemptions reported in early May as AI stocks surged, drawing capital away from the South Korean market. One report highlighted that a surprising country has outperformed U.S. stocks significantly since the start of 2025, though this did not specify South Korea. A recent downgrade suggests further downside is likely for EWY.

headlines.title: The U.S. Market Is On Fire, But One Surprising Country Has Outperformed U.S. Stocks 10-to-1 Since the Start of 2025
EWY: South Korea ETF Plunges - Why More Downside Is Likely (Rating Downgrade) (EWY)
The Zacks Analyst Blog QQQ, DIA, DRAM, WGMI, EWY, DIME and BKCH
Record outflow from South Korean ETF puts focus on the country's best stock performers (EWY:NYSEARCA)
BlackRock South Korea ETF Sees Record Exodus as AI Stocks Soar
